findings.evidence: Grep hits: `BernsteinMeasures.lean:1398` has `hfin : ∀ n, IsFiniteMeasure (σ n)` immediately before `BernsteinMeasures.lean:1399` has the uniform `ofReal` mass bound; Mathlib defines `IsFiniteMeasure` by `measure_univ_lt_top` at `Mathlib/MeasureTheory/Measure/Typeclasses/Finite.lean:35-36`.
findings.fix: Remove `hfin` from the signature and derive the local instance in the proof from `hmass n`, e.g. `haveI : IsFiniteMeasure (σ n) := ⟨(hmass n).trans_lt ENNReal.ofReal_lt_top⟩`.
findings.issue: `kernel_approx_error_tendsto` assumes `hfin : ∀ n, IsFiniteMeasure (σ n)` even though `hmass : ∀ n, (σ n) univ ≤ ENNReal.ofReal C` already implies each `σ n` is finite.

summary: One public lemma carries a redundant finite-measure hypothesis; the mass bound already gives the required finiteness, so the API is stronger than its natural form.
